questions.question: What is a number system?
questions.correct_answer: A standard sequence of symbols, objects or names used to represent numbers
questions.explanation: A number system allows us to count, write and represent numbers in an organised way.

questions.question: What is one-to-one mapping in counting?
questions.correct_answer: Associating each object with exactly one counting symbol or object
questions.explanation: It ensures that every object counted corresponds to one counting mark or symbol.

questions.question: What are tally marks?
questions.correct_answer: Marks used to count objects, where one mark represents one object
questions.explanation: Tally marks were one of the earliest methods of representing numbers.

questions.question: What are Roman numerals?
questions.correct_answer: A number system that uses symbols like I, V, X, L, C, D and M to represent numbers
questions.explanation: This system was used in ancient Rome for writing numbers.

questions.question: What is the Roman numeral for 10?
questions.explanation: In the Roman number system, the symbol X represents the number 10.

questions.question: What is the Roman numeral for 50?
What is meant by landmark numbers in Roman numerals?
questions.correct_answer: Special numbers that have their own symbols in the Roman system

questions.question: What is a base in a number system?
questions.correct_answer: The number that determines how place values increase in a number system
questions.explanation: For example, the decimal system has base 10.

questions.question: What is the base of the Hindu–Arabic number system?
questions.explanation: The decimal number system uses ten digits from 0 to 9.

questions.question: Why is the Hindu–Arabic number system widely used?
questions.correct_answer: Because it is efficient for representing large numbers and performing calculations
questions.explanation: It uses place value and a small set of digits to represent any number.

notes.title: Early Counting Methods
notes.content: Ancient people counted using objects like sticks, pebbles and body parts before written numbers existed.

notes.title: Tally Mark System
notes.content: Tally marks are simple counting marks where each mark represents one object counted.

notes.title: Roman Number System
notes.content: Roman numerals use symbols such as I, V, X, L, C, D and M to represent numbers.

notes.title: Hindu–Arabic Number System
notes.content: This system uses digits 0 to 9 and place value, making it efficient for calculations.

notes.title: Base of a Number System
notes.content: The base determines how place values increase in a number system.

facts.fact: Ancient people used body parts like fingers and toes for counting.
The Roman number system does not use the digit zero.
The Hindu–Arabic number system originated in India.
The decimal number system uses base 10.
